title: Review flow shows blank page on Start Review
description:
User report: the review flow is broken — tapping into the Reviews section and pressing "Start Review" shows a blank page instead of the review session. Should be fixed.

This is the REVIEW-FLOW TWIN of the learn-session blank-screen bug (T106, re-attempted as pending task T112). It is the SAME two-part SwiftUI pattern, in the review-session files rather than the learn-session files — T112 fixes only the LEARN path (MCSessionView + HomeView/DeckDetailView) and does NOT touch these review files, so the review path needs its own fix (or a shared fix).

Confirmed anchors (read the code):
1. Presentation nil-engine capture: Sources/Features/Reviews/ReviewsTabView.swift — `@State private var reviewSessionEngine: SessionEngine?` (line 8), presented via `.fullScreenCover(isPresented: $isShowingReviewSession)` (line 74) whose content closure does `if let reviewSessionEngine { ReviewSessionView(engine: reviewSessionEngine) }` (lines 75-76). `startReviewSession()` (line 229) sets `reviewSessionEngine = try services.study.makeReviewSession()` (line 231) then flips the presenting flag — the same stale/nil-engine-capture shape that blocked the learn path. Start buttons are at lines 113 and 139 (AXID.startReview); there is also a pendingAction==.startReview auto-start path (line 224, driven by the "Start my review" App Intent T103).
2. View-model init-on-else-branch: Sources/Features/Session/ReviewSessionView.swift — `@State private var viewModel: ReviewSessionViewModel?` (line 6), only built inside `.onAppear` on the else-branch placeholder (lines 101-104: `viewModel = ReviewSessionViewModel(engine: engine, services: services)`), which never re-renders into the `if let viewModel` branch (line 33) — identical to the MCSessionView bug.

The fix almost certainly MIRRORS T112 exactly, applied to the review files: (a) in ReviewSessionView.swift build the view-model with a state-safe pattern (non-optional @State/init or a .task on a stable container, not onAppear-on-else-branch), and (b) switch ReviewsTabView.swift from `.fullScreenCover(isPresented:)` to `.fullScreenCover(item:)` keyed on the engine so the cover is never presented with a nil engine. Note ReviewSessionView differs from MCSessionView in card UI (reveal + GradeButtonRow instead of multiple-choice answer rows), but the presentation/init bug is the same.

Why it matters: reviews are a core daily-use flow (the whole spaced-repetition loop); a blank Start Review makes reviewing impossible on device, same severity as the learn-session bug.

Deferred to conversion (do NOT decide here): whether this is a standalone sibling task to T112, whether to wait for/depend on T112 landing first (to copy its proven pattern), or whether the two should be unified; and the exact acceptance bar (should mirror T112: real review session UI renders from Start Review — and ideally from the App Intent auto-start path too — verified on the simulator, screen not blank, close dismisses). Also note there may be an empty-queue consideration: if there are 0 due cards, what SHOULD Start Review show (an empty/all-done state vs a session) — worth conversion clarifying, distinct from the render bug.

title: No way to switch between owned language packs/trips
description:
User report: owning more than one pack (e.g. France AND Japan) offers no way to switch which one you are learning. The Learn tab top-left just shows the single active trip ("Weekend in Tokyo · Day 1") with no control to change to a different owned pack.

Confirmed architecture (read the code) — this is a MODEL gap, not just a missing button:
- The app is fundamentally SINGLE-ACTIVE-TRIP. TripStore (Sources/Persistence/TripStore.swift) exposes only activeTrip() (line 11), saveNewTrip() (line 20), deactivateActiveTrip() (line 57), dayNumber() (line 45) — there is NO allTrips()/listTrips() and NO setActive(trip). Only one trip can be active at a time.
- Owned packs DO persist independently of the active trip: entitlements live in Sources/Persistence/EntitlementStore.swift, and AppState.startNewTrip() (Sources/App/AppState.swift:60) deactivates the active trip but the comment (line 59) states purchases/entitlements "must survive across trips." So the user genuinely owns France + Japan even though only Tokyo is active.
- The Learn header text comes from the active trip: HomeViewModel (Sources/Features/Home/HomeViewModel.swift:28 reads activeTrip()) feeds HomeView subtitle (Sources/Features/Home/HomeView.swift:133 Text(viewModel.subtitle)) = "Weekend in Tokyo · Day 1". Same activeTrip() dependency in ProgressViewModel:40, BrowseViewModel:39-40, ReviewsTabView:240.
- Today the ONLY way to change the language is AppState.startNewTrip() → deactivateActiveTrip() → re-onboarding (TripShapeView etc.), which drops the current Tokyo trip. There is no switch/resume UI anywhere (grep for switchTrip/selectTrip/myTrips/pack picker/library returned nothing).
- The Browse tab currently is NOT a pack library — BrowseView (Sources/Features/Browse/BrowseView.swift) is just a "Browse" header + "Restore purchases" button + restored toast; BrowseViewModel reads activeCountryId from the active trip. So Browse could be a natural home for a pack switcher but isnt one yet.

Why it matters: packs are the paid unit (~£3 each) and the app is pitched around owning packs for different trips; a user who buys multiple packs currently can only ever use the most recently onboarded one without destructively re-onboarding, which likely loses the other trips day-count/context. This undercuts the multi-pack purchase proposition.

Deferred to conversion (do NOT decide here — several real product + model decisions):
- Desired UX: a pack/trip switcher (tap the Learn header? a menu? a new Library/My Packs screen? repurpose the Browse tab?).
- The big model question: does switching RESUME a previously-created trip (requires TripStore to hold MULTIPLE TripProfileRecords with one active + a setActive/allTrips API, and to preserve each trips Day-N and SRS state), or does it just let you START a new trip for another owned pack (simpler, but you lose the old trips day count)? This changes schema/persistence (SwiftData TripProfileRecord — is it currently one-row-only?).
- How per-pack SRS progress (CardSRSRecord) and daily activity map when multiple trips coexist — are cards already namespaced by country/deck, or would concurrent trips collide?
- Whether this is one task or a small epic (model change → store API → switcher UI → header wiring). Likely needs decomposition and possibly a needs-human design decision on the multi-trip model before building.
- Relationship to entitlements: the switcher should only offer packs the user actually owns (EntitlementStore) — and how it interacts with the "Start my review"/learn flows per active pack.
